dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7231 IH: Lobbyist Loophole Closure Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Ramirez (for herself, Ms. Williams of Georgia, Mr. Mullin, and Ms. Simon) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995 to expand the scope of individuals and activities which are subject to the requirements of such Act, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Lobbyist Loophole Closure Act.

section.header: Expanding scope of individuals and activities subject to requirements of Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995

subsection.header: Coverage of individuals providing legislative, political, and strategic counseling services

paragraph.header: Treatment of legislative, political, and strategic counseling services in support of lobbying contacts as lobbying activity
paragraph.text: Section 3(7) of the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995 (2 U.S.C. 1602(7)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking efforts and inserting any efforts; and
by striking research and other background work and inserting the following: counseling in support of such preparation and planning activities, research, and other background work.

paragraph.header: Treatment of lobbying contact made with support of counseling services as lobbying contact made by individual providing services
paragraph.text: Section 3(8) of such Act (2 U.S.C. 1602(8)) is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.header: Treatment of providers of counseling services
subparagraph.text: Any individual, with authority to director or substantially influence any lobbying contact made by another individual, and for financial or other compensation provides counseling services in support of preparation and planning activities which are treated as lobbying activities under paragraph (7) for that other individual’s lobbying contact and who has knowledge that the specific lobbying contact was made, shall be considered to have made the same lobbying contact at the same time in the same manner to the covered executive branch official or covered legislative branch official involved.

subsection.header: Reduction of percentage exemption for determination of threshold of lobbying contacts required for individuals To register as lobbyists
subsection.text: Section 3(10) of such Act (2 U.S.C. 1602(10)) is amended by striking less than 20 percent and inserting less than 10 percent.
The amendments made by this section shall apply with respect to lobbying contacts made on or after the date of the enactment of this Act.
